codeReview.summary: The change stays in scope, but it does not actually implement the required RPC-mode bypass. The runtime treats non-interactive mode as `!ctx.hasUI`, while RPC binds a real extension UI context, so continuation can still fire in RPC sessions. The added test also mis-simulates RPC by reusing the same no-UI harness as print mode, so it cannot catch that regression.

issues.description: `isNonInteractiveContext()` equates non-interactive mode with `!ctx.hasUI`, but RPC mode binds a concrete UI context (`packages/coding-agent/src/modes/rpc/rpc-mode.ts:125,288`), and `ExtensionRunner.hasUI()` returns true whenever the context is not the noop UI (`packages/coding-agent/src/core/extensions/runner.ts:342-343`). As a result, the new guard suppresses continuation in print mode but not in real RPC mode, so the feature does not satisfy the required `--print` / RPC bypass behavior.

The parameterized "print mode" / "rpc mode" cases both call the same `createNonInteractiveHarness()` helper, which simply omits `uiContext` and therefore only exercises `ctx.hasUI === false`. Because actual RPC mode binds a UI context, this test is not an RPC simulation and cannot verify the behavior promised by the feature description and handoff.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The repository has conflicting shared guidance about RPC-mode UI semantics. `ExtensionContext.hasUI` is commented as "false in print/RPC mode", but the real RPC runtime binds an `ExtensionUIContext`, which makes `hasUI()` true. This mismatch is likely what led the worker to implement and test the wrong signal.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `packages/coding-agent/src/core/extensions/types.ts:268` says `hasUI` is false in print/RPC mode, while `packages/coding-agent/src/modes/rpc/rpc-mode.ts:125,288` creates and binds a UI context and `packages/coding-agent/src/core/extensions/runner.ts:342-343` defines `hasUI()` as `this.uiContext !== noOpUIContext`.

summary: Fail: the feature only gates on `ctx.hasUI === false`, which covers print mode but not actual RPC mode, and the added test mislabels a no-UI harness as an RPC simulation so the bug goes undetected.
